As a Software Engineer - AI at Copart, I contribute to the development of innovative solutions such as AI-powered call bots and Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines. These initiatives enhance operational efficiency by automating key processes, reducing manual effort, and improving customer satisfaction. My work incorporates advanced prompt engineering, Flask REST APIs, and LangChain to address business challenges effectively. I hold a Master of Science in Computer Science from the University of Utah, where my research focused on evaluating the temporal reasoning capabilities of Large Language Models (LLMs). I bring expertise in Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), Prompt Engineering, and tools like Huggingface, which align with my passion for leveraging AI to deliver measurable value.

### TransientTables: Evaluating LLMs' Reasoning on Temporally Evolving Semi-structured Tables
...
Humans continuously make new discoveries, and understanding temporal sequence of events leading to these breakthroughs is essential for advancing science and society. This ability to reason over time allows us to identify future steps and understand the effects of financial and political decisions on our lives. However, large language models (LLMs) are typically trained on static datasets, limiting their ability to perform effective temporal reasoning. To assess the temporal reasoning capabilities of LLMs, we present the TRANSIENTTABLES dataset, which comprises 3,971 questions derived from over 14,000 tables, spanning 1,238 entities across multiple time periods. We introduce a template-based question-generation pipeline that harnesses LLMs to refine both templates and questions. Additionally, we establish baseline results using state-of-the-art LLMs to create a benchmark. We also introduce novel modeling strategies centered around task decomposition, enhancing LLM performance. Show less

If you’re wondering where I’ve been: I haven’t been ghosting LinkedIn, I’ve just been deep in the trenches at Copart as an AI/ML Software Engineering Intern. 

Turns out, when you move from the theoretical world of a Master’s at UT Dallas to the production-level scale of Copart, "Day 1" quickly turns into "Month 3". 😮‍💨 

Looking back, there’s a distinct theme to my last year:
...
✈️ Summer 2025: Solving logistics puzzles at American Airlines . 
🏎️ Spring 2026: Architecting intelligent solutions at Copart .
...
Copart has been an incredible place to grow. The culture here is fast-paced, and I’ve been lucky to work with a team that actually trusts their interns with high-impact work. Transitioning from the tarmac to the auction block has been a masterclass in making AI actually work in the real world. It’s been less about the "perfect" algorithm and more about building stable code and pipelines that don’t break when you look at them wrong. 🏗️ 

The biggest takeaway? AI is only as good as the engineering that supports it. I’ve learned how to move from "it works on my machine" to "it works for the enterprise," and that shift has changed how I approach every problem. ✨ 

As I wrap up my final few weeks before graduating mid-May, I’m realizing that as much as I love the student life (and the GoPass discounts), I’m ready to trade the classroom for the next big production-level challenge. 💫 

The momentum is high, the coffee intake is higher, and I’m ready for whatever's next. 🚀 #SoftwareEngineering #AIML #MachineLearning #Python #DataScience #BigData #MLOps #TechHiring #UTD #UTDallas #Comets #Copart #DallasTech #Graduation2026 #EntryLevel #NewGrad #AIEngineer #UTDGrad

results.title: Hemanth Reddy
highlights:
Senior Software Engineer → AI Systems | Agentic Workflows · Multi-Agent Orche
...
Software Engineer at [Copart](https://www.linkedin.com/company/copart)
...
I build AI systems that go beyond demos — production-grade agentic workflows, multi-model orchestration pipelines, and full-stack platforms that handle real load, real users, and real edge cases. Over 6 years I've moved from building large-scale e-commerce frontends to architecting AI agent systems at Copart (one of the world's largest vehicle auction platforms) and shipping Fyntro, an AI-powered trading analysis platform I built from scratch as a side project. What I actually do: → Design multi-agent systems where specialized AI agents collaborate, debate, and synthesize — not just single-prompt wrappers → Build the "connective tissue" between LLMs and live enterprise infrastructure: APIs, legacy data systems, WebSocket pipelines, and event-driven backends → Own end-to-end delivery: from architecture and cloud infra (AWS/Azure) to React frontends and FastAPI backends → Lead cross-functional teams, write technical specs, and drive adoption across engineering orgs Recent work I'm proud of: At Copart, I architected an AI agent orchestration system with domain-specific agents for UI generation, API integration review, and code quality validation — cutting manual review cycles significantly while maintaining production reliability. At Fyntro (personal project, live at fyntro.vercel.app), I built a multi-persona LLM debate engine where 4 parallel AI analyst personas (Bull, Bear, Sentiment, Risk) are synthesized by a Head Trader agent before autonomous trade decisions pass a multi-check risk gate. The agentic copilot runs a 12-iteration tool-calling loop across 30 tools with SSE streaming. Technologies I work with daily: Python (FastAPI) · TypeScript · React/Next.js · LLM APIs (OpenAI, Gemini, Claude) · RAG · AWS · Kafka · Redis · PostgreSQL MS in Computer Science from UT Dallas (3.85 GPA). Based in Dallas, TX — open to hybrid and high-impact remote/travel roles. Always interested in connecting with engineers building serious AI infrastructure.
...
### Software Engineer at [Copart](https://www.linkedin.com/company/copart) (Current)
...
Copart is a $50B+ global vehicle auction platform operating in 11 countries. I work at the intersection of AI tooling, platform engineering, and frontend modernization — leading a team of 6 engineers across multiple workstreams. AI & Agentic Systems Architected and deployed an AI agent orchestration system with Copart-domain-specific agents for UI generation, API integration review, and code quality validation — reducing manual review cycles and accelerating development velocity across teams Built agentic development tooling integrated with Claude Code and Cursor IDE, enabling AI-assisted code generation workflows adopted across the engineering org Platform Engineering & Backend Engineered Spring Boot + Python microservices with Redis caching on AWS ECS, reducing API latency by 50% and maintaining sub-100ms response times under peak global auction load Designed Kafka + REST event-driven architecture to synchronize live auction events and vehicle tracking data in real time, cutting system lag from 5s to under 2s Standardized API/UI performance frameworks across 3 teams via technical specs and cross-team adoption programs, reducing redundant workflows and accelerating delivery by 30% Frontend & Full-Stack Led migration of 40+ legacy Redux components to TypeScript + React Hooks + MUI, adding real-time WebSocket-powered driver/lot tracking and manager assignment workflows — improving load times by 25% Shipped a geospatial capacity management system for UK operations (React + Google Maps API) enabling postal code zone definition, capacity fill rate visualization, and open/booked region tracking in real time Stack: Python

# Associate CASS Data Scientist
...
As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. By blending strong analytical methods with real‑world operational understanding, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our fleet stays safe, efficient, and ready to serve our Customers.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

The AI and Machine Learning industry is experiencing tremendous growth, making this role an excellent long-term career investment. Within the next 2–3 years, professionals can progress into positions such as Machine Learning Engineer, Generative AI Engineer, Data Scientist, AI Solutions Engineer, NLP Engineer, MLOps Engineer, or Full Stack AI Developer. The rapid adoption of Large Language Models, Agentic AI systems, Retrieval-Augmented Generation architectures, and cloud-native AI applications is creating high demand for skilled professionals across industries. Candidates should focus on strengthening Python programming, deep learning, prompt engineering, vector databases, cloud platforms, software architecture, and deployment automation skills. Building expertise in LangChain, LlamaIndex, AWS, Kubernetes, and modern AI frameworks can significantly increase career opportunities and earning potential in both Indian and global technology markets.

In this role, you will be instrumental in designing and executing our artificial intelligence strategy to elevate our developer experience platform to unprecedented heights.

You'll leverage your expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and large language models to solve complex business challenges and drive measurable impact across the organization.

As a Data Scientist at arenaflex, you will be responsible for driving our AI initiatives and contributing to the development of next-generation developer tools. Your key responsibilities will include:
...
Design, develop, and implement machine learning models and algorithms that enhance our developer experience platform, ensuring alignment with arenaflex's overall technology strategy.
...

...
Choose appropriate machine learning algorithms, train models, and calibrate hyperparameters to achieve optimal predictive performance and model accuracy.
...
Apply deep expertise in transformer architecture, vector embeddings, and advanced ML techniques to develop solutions that solve complex business challenges.
...
Lead model deployment for experimentation, implementing A/B testing, monitoring model performance, and iterating on solutions based on empirical results.
...
Work closely with AI engineers, software developers, product managers, and other data scientists to deliver end-to-end AI-enabled solutions.
...
Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a
...
technical field. A Master's degree is strongly preferred and will be given special consideration.
...
Minimum of 5+ years of progressive experience in the software industry with a focused specialization in machine learning and artificial intelligence.
...
Strong understanding and extensive experience with cutting-edge machine learning algorithms, deep learning architectures, statistical modeling, and large language models.
...
- Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field

- Experience applying machine learning techniques specifically in developer productivity or software development lifecycle contexts

- Knowledge of cloud platforms and infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

- Familiarity with MLOps practices and deployment pipelines

- Published research or contributions to open-source machine learning projects

- Experience with prompt engineering and LLM optimization techniques
...
If you are passionate about artificial intelligence, machine learning, and want to work on projects that will shape the future of technology, arenaflex is the place for you. We are looking for individuals who bring enthusiasm, dedication, and fresh perspectives to their work – people who are ready to push boundaries and achieve extraordinary results.
